        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)}), children={n['children']}")

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After pressing SPACE:
- Player: N at (28,40)-(30,43) - same position
- White strip: W at (31,40)-(31,43) - now a horizontal strip at row 31
- New object: G at (32,40)-(35,43) with child b at (33,41)-(34,42) - this is the carried square!
So pressing SPACE separated the white strip from the carried square! The white strip is now just a 1x4 horizontal strip at row 31, and the carried square is now a separate object below it at rows 32-35.
This is interesting. The white strip is now detached from the carried square. The carried square is at (32,40)-(35,43), which is below the white strip.
Now I need to figure out how to merge the carried square with the merged object. The merged object is at (24,44)-(31,47), and the carried square is at (32,40)-(35,43).
Let me try moving RIGHT to push the carried square into the merged object. But wait, the player is at (28,40)-(30,43), and the merged object is at (24,44)-(31,47). The player is blocking the path to the right.
Let me try moving UP first to get out of the way, then move the carried square into the merged object.
Actually, looking at the positions:
- Merged object: (24,44)-(31,47)
- Carried square: (32,40)-(35,43)
- Player: (28,40)-(30,43)
The carried square is at rows 32-35, cols 40-43. The merged object is at rows 24-31, cols 44-47. They're adjacent at the corner: carried square's top-right is (32, 43), merged object's bottom-left is (31, 44).
If I move the carried square UP and RIGHT, it would merge with the merged object. But how do I move the carried square? The carried square seems to move with the player.
Let me try moving UP to see if the carried square moves with the player.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Carried square separated from white strip. Try moving UP to move carried square.
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
